name: Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116)
name_original: Tarancón - Belinchón – circular por el sendero de la Vía Negrín (PR-CU 116)
region_intro: Hiking around Tarancón offers routes through a landscape characterized by gently rolling terrain, agricultural fields, and established regional paths. The area features low elevation changes, making many trails accessible for various fitness levels. These routes often connect small towns and historical sites, providing a blend of natural and cultural exploration. The region's trails are generally well-defined, traversing open countryside and occasional wooded sections. Best hiking trails around Tarancón The most popular hiking route is Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12), an 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route starts and ends in Santa Cruz de la Zarza. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116), a difficult 13.9 miles (22.3 km) path. This route connects the towns of Tarancón and Belinchón via a named regional path. Local hikers also love the Camino Santiaguista de Uclés, an 11.2 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through varied countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Tarancón is defined by open countryside, agricultural landscapes, and historical paths, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Tarancón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Tarancón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tarancón?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available around Tarancón, offering a variety of experiences. These include 18 easy trails, 13 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Tarancón?
A: The hiking trails around Tarancón are generally accessible, with a significant number of easy and moderate routes. You'll find 18 easy trails and 13 moderate options, making the area suitable for casual walkers and those looking for a bit more challenge. There is also one difficult route for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Tarancón?
A: Yes, Tarancón offers many easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.5 km route with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a gentle introduction to the area's landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes near Tarancón?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature of hiking in Tarancón. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018344745/sendero-del-charco-negro-circular-from-santa-cruz-de-la-zarza-pr-to-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Charco Negro – circular from Santa Cruz de la Zarza (PR-TO 12)&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.4-mile (13.6 km) moderate loop that starts and ends in Santa Cruz de la Zarza.

Q: What are some longer hiking options for experienced hikers in Tarancón?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013706425/tarancon-belinchon-circular-via-the-negrin-path-pr-cu-116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón - Belinchón - circular via the Negrín Path (PR-CU 116)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.9-mile (22.3 km) path connecting the towns of Tarancón and Belinchón. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146797921/camino-santiaguista-de-Uclés" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Santiaguista de Uclés&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.2-mile (18.1 km) trail.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Tarancón?
A: Hikes around Tarancón feature a landscape of gently rolling terrain, extensive agricultural fields, and established regional paths. You'll primarily experience open countryside with occasional wooded sections, connecting small towns and historical sites. The low elevation changes make for generally comfortable walking.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking near Tarancón?
A: The region around Tarancón is rich in history. You can explore landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of the Order of Santiago – Gabriela and Lorenzo Rest Area&lt;/a&gt;, the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz de la Zarza&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5210277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de la Malena and Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Uclés&lt;/a&gt; itself is a historical route.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Tarancón?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Tarancón are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers crisp air, but trails might be cooler and require appropriate layering.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Tarancón suitable for dogs?
A: While specific regulations for each trail can vary, the open countryside and agricultural paths around Tarancón generally offer a pleasant environment for walking with dogs. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Tarancón?
A: Yes, many of the easy and moderate trails around Tarancón are suitable for families. The low elevation changes and well-defined paths make them accessible for children. Shorter loop trails, like the 6.5 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;, are excellent choices for a family outing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tarancón?
A: The trails in Tarancón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet, open countryside, the blend of natural and cultural exploration, and the well-defined paths that connect charming towns and historical sites.

Q: Are there options for parking at the trailheads in Tarancón?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many routes around Tarancón, especially those starting from towns like Santa Cruz de la Zarza or Tarancón itself, offer accessible parking options. It's generally easy to find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for these regional paths.
tips: Tarancón offers several places to visit and among them the Plaza de la Constitución next to the town hall, the Malena arch and, just behind, the church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción stand out. On the other hand, the sculptures of Don Quixote inside the palace of the Dukes of Riánsares and within it some caves that are worth visiting.

On the outskirts of the quiet town of Belinchón is this small hermitage that venerates San Cristóbal and is an ideal place to take a break on your route and enjoy this La Mancha landscape and the tranquility that surrounds it.

waypoints.name: Arco de la Malena and Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, Tarancón
